architecture.summary: Rational Sparse Autoencoder (RSAE) replaces fixed encoder nonlinearities (ReLU, JumpReLU, TopK) in standard SAEs with a trainable rational function. A two-stage pipeline initializes rational coefficients via relaxed Remez exchange on synthetic data, calibrates scale parameters, then fine-tunes under sparsity-regularized reconstruction loss. The rational activation uniformly approximates existing SAE activation primitives while adapting to pre-activation geometry.

claims.statement: Rational activations are flexible enough to uniformly approximate the activation primitives used by existing SAE families on compact domains
RSAE strictly improves on baseline SAEs after the fine-tuning step, both on reconstruction-side metrics and on downstream-behaviour metrics
RSAE gains are consistent across host language models, across baseline activation families, and across the full range of baseline sparsity tested
Feature-level interpretability is preserved under sparse probing
The upgrade adds only a handful of scalar parameters per autoencoder and runs in minutes on a single consumer GPU
